sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Perfromance Wealth Partners LLC Has $61.61 Million Stock Position in GE Vernova Inc. $GEV
feed.summary: Performance Wealth Partners LLC increased its stake in GE Vernova Inc. by 1.5% to 94,271 shares, now valued at $61.61 million, making it the firm's 11th largest holding. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in GEV. GE Vernova reported strong earnings of $13.39 EPS against an expected $2.99 and revenue of $10.96 billion, with Wall Street analysts largely recommending a "Buy" rating and an average price target of $863.61.

feed.title: Perfromance Wealth Partners LLC Purchases 14,757 Shares of Bank of America Corporation $BAC
feed.summary: Perfromance Wealth Partners LLC increased its stake in Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) by 36.3% in the fourth quarter, acquiring an additional 14,757 shares, bringing their total holdings to 55,373 shares valued at approximately $3.045 million. This comes despite recent insider selling, with Bernard A. Mensah and Thomas M. Scrivener offloading shares worth millions. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus for BAC, with an average price target of $58.90, though some firms have recently adjusted their targets.

feed.title: Perfromance Wealth Partners LLC Reduces Holdings in KKR & Co. Inc. $KKR
feed.summary: Perfromance Wealth Partners LLC reduced its stake in KKR & Co. Inc. by 8.5% in Q4 2025, selling 16,393 shares and retaining 175,958 shares valued at $22.43 million. Despite this reduction, significant insider buying occurred on February 17th, 2026, with CEO Joseph Y. Bae and Director Matt Cohler acquiring substantial shares. Institutional ownership remains high at 76.26%, and analysts have a "Moderate Buy" rating with a consensus target price of $141.20 for KKR, which trades near $90.62.

feed.title: Republic Services opens expanded recycling facility in Massachusetts
feed.summary: Republic Services Inc. (NYSE: RSG) has opened its upgraded Peabody Recycling Center in Massachusetts, featuring advanced technology to enhance recycling capacity. The 54,000-square-foot facility incorporates automated sorting, AI, and improved safety systems, capable of processing 35-40 tons of recyclables per hour. This expansion aims to increase material diversion from landfills and provide cleaner commodities to end-users across greater Boston.

feed.title: Oscar Mayer Unveils First Bacon Innovation in Five Years with New Maple Bourbon Bacon
feed.summary: Oscar Mayer has introduced its first new bacon product in five years, Maple Bourbon Bacon, which is cured with Evan Williams Bourbon. This innovation aims to meet consumer demand for new, flavorful bacon options, incorporating sweet, smoky, and savory notes. The brand also plans to launch additional limited-edition bacon flavors annually and new hot dog varieties for the summer grilling season.

feed.title: Exxon Mobil Corporation stock: Strong amid oil shocks—what you need to know now
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil's stock is near record highs due to geopolitical oil surges, but a recent after-hours drop signals caution. The company boasts a diversified business model, consistent dividend growth, and strategic projects positioning it for future growth, despite concerns about narrowing refining margins and long-term energy transition shifts. Investors should monitor Q1 2026 earnings, oil prices, and analyst reports to determine entry points.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Adjusts Price Target on General Mills to $33 From $35, Maintains Underweight Rating
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has lowered its price target for General Mills (GIS) to $33 from $35, while maintaining an "Underweight" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects the continued analytical scrutiny and re-evaluation of the consumer food giant's future performance by financial institutions. The article briefly mentions other recent analyst actions and General Mills' business activities.

feed.title: Why the U.S. Navy’s retail business is fighting Walmart and Amazon to fund its own future
feed.summary: The U.S. Navy’s retail business, Nexcom, is struggling to compete with giants like Walmart and Amazon, leading to declining sales and reduced funding for crucial morale, welfare, and recreation (MWR) programs for service members and their families. To counter this, Nexcom has embarked on an ambitious turnaround plan, investing in store modernizations, improving merchandise curation, and enhancing the shopping experience to attract and retain military customers. Despite falling sales over the past decade, recent efforts show a slight improvement in customer satisfaction and sales growth, indicating the potential effectiveness of their "Store of the Future" initiative.

feed.title: Henry Schein to Reduce Size of the Board of Directors following 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders
feed.summary: Henry Schein, Inc. announced plans to reduce the size of its Board of Directors from fifteen to ten members following its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders on May 21, 2026. Stanley M. Bergman will retire from the Board after 44 years of service and will be designated Chairman Emeritus. Additionally, Joseph L. Herring, Robert J. Hombach, Scott Serota, and Bradley T. Sheares will not seek re-election.

feed.title: Republic Services Celebrates Opening of Expanded Peabody Recycling Center
feed.summary: Republic Services announced the grand opening of its expanded Peabody Recycling Center in Massachusetts. This state-of-the-art facility features advanced technology, including automated sorting and AI-powered systems, to significantly increase processing capacity to 35-40 tons of recyclables per hour. The upgrade aims to enhance recycling efforts in the greater Boston area, divert more waste from landfills, and produce cleaner materials for end markets, supporting Massachusetts' waste-reduction goals.

feed.title: Dow Inc. and Lyondell Stocks Plunge. Why Iran Could Spark a Brutal Chemicals Selloff.
feed.summary: Commodity chemical stocks, including Dow Inc. and Lyondell, have seen a recent surge but their fortunes appear to be changing. This shift comes after a two-week ceasefire agreement between the U.S. and Iran, contingent on Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz, which could potentially lead to a significant selloff in the chemicals sector.

feed.title: Delta leads the airline sector higher after earnings, oil price decline (DAL:NYSE)
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines (DAL) stock rose in premarket trading after the company reported strong first-quarter results, with revenue up 12.9% year-over-year to $15.9 billion, surpassing analyst estimates. The airline also saw healthy unit revenue improvement across all geographies, including a positive inflection in its main cabin. The overall airline sector is benefiting from Delta's positive earnings report and a decline in oil prices.

feed.title: Texas Pacific Land Corporation $TPL Holdings Lifted by Hunter Associates Investment Management LLC
feed.summary: Hunter Associates Investment Management LLC significantly increased its stake in Texas Pacific Land (TPL) by 199.6% in Q4, now owning 12,198 shares valued at $3.503 million. The company recently reported strong quarterly earnings, beating estimates with $1.79 EPS and $211.6 million in revenue, and raised its quarterly dividend to $0.60. Analyst sentiment remains mixed, with KeyCorp maintaining an "Overweight" rating and a target of $639, while Wall Street overall has a "Hold" consensus.

feed.title: Synopsys, Inc. $SNPS Shares Purchased by Hunter Associates Investment Management LLC
feed.summary: Hunter Associates Investment Management LLC significantly increased its stake in Synopsys, Inc. (NASDAQ:SNPS) by 70.5%, acquiring an additional 2,514 shares. This brings their total holdings to 6,078 shares, valued at approximately $2.853 million. The company recently surpassed Q4 EPS estimates and reported strong revenue growth, though analyst sentiment remains mixed with an average "Hold" rating.

feed.title: Hunter Associates Investment Management LLC Acquires 7,194 Shares of RTX Corporation $RTX
feed.summary: Hunter Associates Investment Management LLC significantly increased its stake in RTX Corporation by 111.7%, adding 7,194 shares to hold a total of 13,637 shares valued at approximately $2.50 million. This move comes despite substantial insider selling totaling over $18 million in the past 90 days. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for RTX, citing a positive business outlook including a $3.8 billion F135 contract modification for Pratt & Whitney.

feed.title: Software company ServiceNow’s investment team shrinks with Kirk departure
feed.summary: Philip Kirk, Senior Vice-President of corporate and business development at ServiceNow, has left the company after nearly six years, marking a further reduction in ServiceNow's investment team. His departure follows that of Vishal Kumar Gupta, VP of corporate development and ventures, less than six months ago. ServiceNow Ventures, launched in 2015, plans to increase its CVC budget from $300m to $1bn and has recently participated in significant funding rounds for AI technology companies.

feed.title: Jericho Financial LLP Buys 1,116 Shares of Costco Wholesale Corporation $COST
feed.summary: Jericho Financial LLP increased its stake in Costco (COST) by 30.5% in Q4, acquiring 1,116 additional shares to hold a total of 4,777 shares valued at approximately $4.12 million. Other institutional investors also modified their holdings, while analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $1,039.13. Costco reported strong earnings, beating estimates with EPS of $4.58 and revenue of $69.6 billion, although insider selling and valuation concerns are noted.

feed.title: Caterpillar Inc. $CAT Stock Holdings Reduced by Jericho Financial LLP
feed.summary: Jericho Financial LLP has reduced its stake in Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT) by 16.4%, now holding 11,125 shares valued at $6.37 million. Despite this, Caterpillar recently exceeded quarterly earnings expectations with an EPS of $5.16 and revenue of $19.13 billion, leading to a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $737.55. Other institutional investors, however, significantly increased their holdings in Caterpillar during the third quarter.

feed.title: BD Delivers Next‑Generation TIPS Innovation to Advance Portal Hypertension Care Across the European Union
feed.summary: BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) announced the CE Mark application for its Liverty™ TIPS Stent Graft, introducing a next-generation, adjustable-diameter device for portal hypertension care in the European Union. This innovation aims to expand treatment options for patients with advanced liver disease and cirrhosis. Safety and effectiveness data from the pivotal ARCH clinical trial for the Liverty™ TIPS Stent Graft will be presented for the first time at the Society of Interventional Radiology Annual Scientific Meeting on April 12, 2026.

feed.title: Citigroup Adjusts PT on Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies to $292 From $283, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies (WAB) to $292, up from $283, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated outlook from the analyst firm for the heavy machinery and vehicles supplier. Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies Corporation is a leading global provider of equipment, systems, and services for the transportation sector, specializing in rail freight and passenger transport.

feed.title: Citigroup Adjusts Price Target on ResMed to $340 From $345, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Citigroup has adjusted its price target for ResMed (RMD) to $340, down from $345, while reiterating a "Buy" rating on the stock. The article, primarily a brief announcement, does not provide details on the reasons for the target price modification but confirms Citigroup's sustained positive outlook for the company's shares. ResMed specializes in medical devices and software for respiratory disorders.

feed.title: 11 Best New Dollar Tree Spring Finds Flying Off Shelves Right Now
feed.summary: This article highlights 11 of the best new spring items available at Dollar Tree, all priced at $5 or less. The finds cover various categories including home decor, garden accessories, outdoor fun, and personal care. Shoppers can discover bargains like hand-painted gnomes, pickleball sets, spring candles, and unique planters.

feed.title: BD’s new EU liver stent adjusts from 6 to 10 mm for tailored care
feed.summary: BD (Becton, Dickinson and Company) has received CE Mark approval for its Liverty™ TIPS Stent Graft, a device with an adjustable diameter (6-10 mm) designed to treat complications of portal hypertension across the European Union. This next-generation stent aims to personalize therapy for patients with cirrhosis and related conditions. Safety and effectiveness data from the pivotal ARCH clinical trial for this device will be presented at the Society of Interventional Radiology Annual Meeting on April 12, 2026.

feed.title: Waters Corp stock: What investors need to know in today's market
feed.summary: Waters Corp, a leader in analytical instruments like chromatography and mass spectrometry, offers stability for investors due to its essential role in drug discovery, material science, and environmental testing. The company's diversified portfolio, global footprint, and recurring revenue model from consumables provide a competitive edge. While facing risks like supply chain disruptions, its strong market position in critical industries makes it an attractive long-term investment, particularly with ongoing global R&D spending and emerging market growth.

feed.title: Barclays Adjusts Price Target on Masco to $65 From $71, Maintains Equalweight Rating
feed.summary: Barclays has adjusted its price target for Masco Corporation (MAS) to $65, down from $71, while maintaining an Equalweight rating on the stock. This adjustment comes alongside similar movements from other financial institutions, as analysts continue to recalibrate their outlooks for the construction supplies and fixtures company. Masco Corporation is a leading manufacturer of home improvement products, including plumbing equipment and architectural decoration products, with its primary market in North America.

feed.title: Corning and Meta start construction on North Carolina AI cable facility
feed.summary: Corning Incorporated and Meta Platforms have begun construction on an expansion of Corning's optical cable manufacturing facility in Hickory, North Carolina. This project aims to support advanced AI data centers with US-developed technology and is part of a multiyear agreement worth up to $6 billion between the two companies. The expansion is expected to strengthen domestic manufacturing, create 15-20% more skilled jobs, and advance US innovation in AI infrastructure.

feed.title: Delta Expects Strong Profit Despite Higher Fuel Costs
feed.summary: Delta expects to achieve a pre-tax profit of about $1 billion in the current quarter, despite projecting an additional $2 billion in fuel costs due to rising jet fuel prices from the war with Iran. The airline plans to cut 3.5% of its flights to mitigate these costs, recovering up to half of the increase through reduced capacity and higher fares. This forecast highlights strong demand for air travel, even with increased ticket prices.

feed.title: Exxon Sees 6% of Its Worldwide Output Shut on Mideast Conflict
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil Corp. reported that 6% of its global first-quarter production was halted due to the conflict in the Middle East, which impacted the Persian Gulf energy industry. Half of these production outages occurred at a liquefied natural gas complex in Qatar, where Exxon is a partner, with two LNG production lines being damaged.

feed.title: Chubb-led maritime reinsurance facility expands to $40bn
feed.summary: The United States International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) and Chubb have expanded their maritime reinsurance facility to $40 billion, with the addition of six new American insurance partners. This initiative, designed to restore maritime trade through the Strait of Hormuz, now includes Travelers, Liberty Mutual Insurance, Berkshire Hathaway, AIG, Starr, and CNA. Chubb will act as the lead underwriter, managing the facility, determining pricing, assuming risk, issuing policies, and handling claims for eligible vessels and cargo.

feed.title: Pacific Point Advisors LLC Buys New Shares in Stryker Corporation $SYK
feed.summary: Pacific Point Advisors LLC reported buying a new stake of 3,348 shares in Stryker Corporation (NYSE:SYK) during the fourth quarter, valued at approximately $1.177 million. This new institutional investment comes as several other hedge funds adjusted their positions in Stryker, and while Director Ronda E. Stryker sold a significant number of shares, institutional ownership remains high at 77.09%. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for Stryker, with an average price target of $425.56.

feed.title: ResMed Inc. $RMD Shares Sold by Pacific Point Advisors LLC
feed.summary: Pacific Point Advisors LLC significantly reduced its stake in ResMed Inc. (NYSE:RMD) by 75.7% in Q4, selling 4,726 shares and retaining 1,515 shares valued at $365,000. Despite this sell-off, ResMed exceeded quarterly earnings and revenue estimates, prompting analysts to raise price targets, with the company currently holding a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating and a $299.27 target. Insider selling by the CEO and a director was also noted, although institutional ownership remains high at 54.98%.

feed.title: Pacific Point Advisors LLC Takes $1.14 Million Position in NextEra Energy, Inc. $NEE
feed.summary: Pacific Point Advisors LLC has acquired a new stake of 14,239 shares in NextEra Energy, Inc. (NYSE:NEE), valued at approximately $1.14 million. This move comes as NextEra Energy recently increased its quarterly dividend to $0.6232 per share and reported strong Q4 EPS, though revenue missed estimates. Several other institutional investors have also adjusted their positions in the utility provider, while company insiders have sold a significant amount of shares over the past 90 days.

feed.title: Coeptis Wins Nasdaq Nod for Z Squared Merger Listing
feed.summary: Coeptis Therapeutics Holdings (COEP) announced that Nasdaq has approved the listing of the post-merger company's common stock under the ticker "ZSQR" following its planned merger with digital infrastructure miner Z Squared Inc. This approval satisfies a key closing condition for the transaction, expected in Q2 2026, after which the combined entity will operate as Z Squared Inc. and focus on institutional-scale Dogecoin and Litecoin mining. Coeptis Therapeutics Holdings is also a biopharmaceutical and technology company developing cell therapy platforms and AI-powered tools.

feed.title: Cinemark (NYSE: CNK) CEO trust returns 109K shares under 10b5-1 plan
feed.summary: Cinemark Holdings, Inc. CEO Sean Gamble, through his joint revocable trust, disposed of 109,455 shares of common stock back to the company at a weighted average price of $30.02 per share on April 6, 2026. This transaction was conducted automatically under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on September 10, 2025. Following the disposition, the trust indirectly holds 585,562 shares, and Gamble directly holds 219,284 shares.

feed.title: CHS Inc. announces quarterly dividends on preferred stock series By Investing.com
feed.summary: CHS Inc. has declared regular quarterly dividends for its various series of preferred stock, payable on June 30, 2026, to shareholders of record as of June 15, 2026. Dividends range from $0.421875 to $0.50 per share, based on a $25.00 per share basis. Additionally, Daniel Schurr resigned from the CHS board of directors due to his appointment to the Nationwide Mutual Insurance Company board.

feed.title: Cross Country promotes exec to chief operating officer
feed.summary: Cross Country Healthcare has promoted Amiee Hawkins to Chief Operating Officer. This move follows a series of other leadership changes at the company, including the return of founder Kevin Clark as CEO and the departures of other executives. The company also recently canceled its proposed merger with Aya Healthcare.

feed.title: WING: TD Cowen Adjusts Wingstop's Price Target, Maintains 'Hold' Rating | WING Stock News
feed.summary: TD Cowen analyst Andrew M. Charles has reaffirmed a 'Hold' rating for Wingstop (WING) but significantly reduced the price target from $285.00 to $175.00. This adjustment follows several other analysts also revising their ratings and price targets for Wingstop, with an average target price from 28 analysts at $302.80, suggesting an 82.36% upside from the current price, while GuruFocus estimates a 176.09% upside based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Downgrades Cogent Communications Holdings (CCOI)
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has downgraded Cogent Communications Holdings (CCOI). The article, sourced from MSN, indicates this change in rating from the financial institution.

feed.title: Cango Inc. Announces March 2026 Operational Update; Strategically Optimizing Mining Fleet and Improving Production Economics
feed.summary: Cango Inc. has released its operational update for March 2026, detailing a strategy focused on optimizing its Bitcoin mining operations for cash margin rather than pure scale. The company has refined its mining fleet, decommissioned inefficient miners, and is migrating capacity to lower-cost power regions, resulting in a significant reduction in cash cost per coin. Additionally, Cango completed a strategic sale of Bitcoins to retire debt, strengthening its balance sheet for its transition into energy and AI infrastructure.

feed.title: Wells Fargo cuts Canadian Solar stock price target on restructuring By Investing.com
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has reduced its price target for Canadian Solar (NASDAQ: CSIQ) from $23 to $17, while maintaining an Equal Weight rating, citing the company's recently announced U.S. restructuring and a new sum-of-the-parts valuation. Despite the stock trading below its fair value, the firm remains cautious due to uncertainties surrounding foreign entity of concern regulations and antidumping risks. Canadian Solar recently reported a fourth-quarter 2025 net loss of $86 million on $1.22 billion revenue and has seen its price targets adjusted by other analysts.

feed.title: Winners And Losers Of Q4: SLB (NYSE:SLB) Vs The Rest Of The Oilfield Services Stocks
feed.summary: This article analyzes the Q4 performance of SLB and other oilfield services companies, highlighting top performers like Borr Drilling and Baker Hughes, and weaker performers such as World Kinect. It discusses the industry's strong Q4 with revenues beating estimates and also touches upon broader market updates, including shifts from AI and crypto narratives to geopolitical risks.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Reassess Phillips 66 (PSX) After Its 95% One Year Surge?
feed.summary: Phillips 66 (PSX) has surged 95% over the last year, leading investors to question its current valuation. Simply Wall St's analysis, using DCF and P/E ratio methods, suggests the stock is currently undervalued by 56.6% and shows potential for further upside based on a "Fair Ratio" of 23.44x. The article further explores various "Narratives," including bull and bear cases, offering different fair value estimations for the company.

feed.title: Nutanix, NetApp form strategic alliance with new integration for modern cloud platform
feed.summary: Nutanix and NetApp have formed a strategic alliance to integrate NetApp's Intelligent Data Infrastructure with the Nutanix Cloud Platform, aiming to provide customers with enhanced flexibility and optimised strategies for virtualisation and data management across various environments. This collaboration seeks to simplify virtualized environment modernization, accelerate VM migration, streamline operations, and offer robust built-in cyber resilience. Future plans include integrating NetApp ONTAP into Nutanix's Agentic AI solution to further accelerate AI innovation.

feed.title: Aetna Provider Survey Reveals Optimism and Opportunities to Simplify Health Care
feed.summary: An inaugural Aetna Provider Survey indicates significant optimism for the future of healthcare, with 60% of respondents believing the system will be less burdensome in five years. While some friction points remain, particularly administrative burdens, the survey highlights opportunities to improve provider-payer trust and leverage technology, such as AI, to simplify processes and enhance patient care. Aetna aims to lead in simplifying prior authorizations and integrating digital solutions to achieve operational excellence and better health outcomes.

feed.title: Roth MKM Downgrades Diamondback Energy(FANG.US) to Hold Rating, Raises Target Price to $200
feed.summary: Roth MKM has downgraded Diamondback Energy (FANG.US) to a Hold rating, moving it from a previous Buy rating. Despite the downgrade, the firm increased its target price for FANG.US to $200, signaling a continued positive outlook on the company's valuation.

feed.title: Is Broadridge (BR) Offering A Chance After Its Recent 27% Share Price Slide?
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR) has experienced a 27.5% share price decline year-to-date, making some investors wonder if it presents a buying opportunity. Both Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis and P/E ratio comparison suggest the stock is undervalued, with analysts projecting significant free cash flow growth. The article recommends exploring these valuation approaches further to understand Broadridge's potential.

feed.title: Costco Energy Drinks Put Pressure On Suppliers And Stretch Valuation Concerns
feed.summary: Costco (NasdaqGS:COST) has launched a new Kirkland Signature energy drink line, directly competing with popular brands like Celsius and potentially impacting its suppliers. This move leverages Costco's private label strategy to apply price pressure on competitors and gain more influence over beverage suppliers, despite the company's shares appearing overvalued by Simply Wall St's metrics. The development highlights Costco's focus on value and could affect P/E ratios and category margins.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines announces March quarter 2026 financial results
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines (NYSE: DAL) reported its March quarter 2026 financial results, with earnings exceeding initial guidance due to strong demand and better-than-expected revenue. Despite a significant increase in fuel costs, the company delivered a pre-tax profit forecast of $1 billion for the June quarter. Delta CEO Ed Bastian highlighted the strength of their brand and financial foundation, emphasizing actions to manage capacity and recapture higher fuel costs.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines Announces March Quarter 2026 Financial Results
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines delivered strong financial results for the March quarter 2026, with earnings more than 40 percent higher than the previous year, despite increased fuel costs. The company reported a March quarter record for total revenue at $14.2 billion, driven by strong demand across corporate and leisure segments. Delta forecasts low-teens revenue growth for the June quarter on flat capacity, expecting around $1 billion in pre-tax profit, and continues to strengthen its investment-grade balance sheet.

feed.title: Mosaic to idle two Brazil sites, cutting 1M tonnes of phosphate
feed.summary: Mosaic (NYSE: MOS) announced it will idle and demobilize its Araxá Mining and Chemical Complex and related mining activities at Patrocínio, Brazil, leading to workforce reductions and a reduction of approximately 1 million tonnes in annual phosphate output. The company plans to pursue a sale of the Araxá assets while continuing Niobium development at Patrocínio. This strategic move is expected to result in a pre-tax book impact of $350-$400 million in Q1 2026, including a significant impairment, but also projected annual declines in capital and operating expenses post-transaction.
